1	{
2	  "body": "Uberon has:

[foramen secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006678) [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) [atrial septum secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004155)

NCIT has:

[Septum Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34295) [Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_R82) [Foramen Secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C34179)

The 'part of' relationship goes in the opposite direction.

Uberon contains mappings to the respective NCIT terms. Which is correct?",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd86sXUwY",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "rays22"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "Good catch.
I am afraid neither the current Uberon nor the NCIT 'part of' relationship is correct.

1. Uberon [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) has:
    * ~'foramen secundum part of atrial septum secundum'~ --> **FALSE!!**
        * CORRECTLY: [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) **'part of' some 'atrial septum primum'**
            * The corrected definition is based on the description "*the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen in the septum primum*" in the wikipedia article on [Foramen secundum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foramen_secundum).
            * The corrected definition is also supported by the medical anatomy textbook ISBN: 9632412311 that I have at hand.
2.  NCIT [NCIT:C34295 Septum Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34295) - [NCIT:C34179 Foramen Secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/ncit/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FNCIT_C34179?lang=en) relationship:
    * ~Septum Secundum Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of Foramen Secundum~ --> also **FALSE!!**
    *This relationship is incorrect for multiple reasons:
        * It reverses the actual anatomical relationship - a septum (which is a wall or partition) cannot be part of a foramen (which is an opening or hole).
        * Neither structure is physically part of the other - they are distinct entities with specific developmental and spatial relationships:
            * the septum secundum is the second septal structure that grows down from the roof of the atrium
            * the foramen secundum is an opening that forms in the septum primum (not in the septum secundum)
        * The correct developmental relationship is that the foramen secundum forms in the septum primum, while the foramen ovale forms in the septum secundum. These are separate structures that work together to create a one-way valve for fetal blood circulation.

I will correct the EQ of [UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0006678) to:
* (['anatomical conduit'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004111?lang=en) and 'part of' some ['atrial septum primum'](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols4/ontologies/uberon/classes/http%253A%252F%252Fpurl.obolibrary.org%252Fobo%252FUBERON_0004154))",
11	      "createdAt": "2025-05-19T17:37:50Z",
12	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
13	      "isMinimized": false,
14	      "minimizedReason": "",
15	      "reactionGroups": [],
16	      "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522#issuecomment-2891795480",
17	      "viewerDidAuthor": false
18	    }
19	  ],
20	  "title": "relationship is reversed between Uberon and NCIT for foramen secundum",
21	  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522"
22	}

[Term]
id: UBERON:0004154
name: atrial septum primum
alt_id: UBERON:0004157
def: "In the developing heart, the cavity of the primitive atrium becomes subdivided into right and left chambers by a septum, the septum primum, which grows downward into the cavity. The increasingly smaller gap below it (before it fuses with the endocardial cushion) is known as the ostium primum. The septum primum eventually fuses with the endocardial cushion, closing the ostium primum off completely. Meanwhile, perforations appear in the superior part of the septum primum, forming the ostium secundum. This will eventually form part of the fossa ovalis." [GO:0003284]

[Term]
id: UBERON:0004154
name: atrial septum primum
alt_id: UBERON:0004157
def: "In the developing heart, the cavity of the primitive atrium becomes subdivided into right and left chambers by a septum, the septum primum, which grows downward into the cavity. The increasingly smaller gap below it (before it fuses with the endocardial cushion) is known as the ostium primum. The septum primum eventually fuses with the endocardial cushion, closing the ostium primum off completely. Meanwhile, perforations appear in the superior part of the septum primum, forming the ostium secundum. This will eventually form part of the fossa ovalis." [GO:0003284]

comment: The septum secundum, semilunar in shape, grows downward from the upper wall of the atrium immediately to the right of the primary septum and ostium secundum. git add src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix part_of relationship for foramen secundum (UBERON:0006678)

Issue obophenotype/uberon#3522: the foramen secundum is incorrectly
asserted as part_of atrial septum secundum (UBERON:0004155). The
foramen secundum is actually a perforation in the septum primum
(UBERON:0004154), as documented in:

  - Wikipedia: "the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen
    in the septum primum"
  - The atrial septum primum (UBERON:0004154) own definition:
    "perforations appear in the superior part of the septum primum,
    forming the ostium secundum"
  - The existing external_definition on UBERON:0006678 already
    describes it as one "of the two openings to perforate the septum
    primum"

Changes:
  - Corrected text def from "A foramen in the septum secundum" to
    "A foramen in the septum primum".
  - Corrected logical def part_of UBERON:0004155 (atrial septum
    secundum) to UBERON:0004154 (atrial septum primum).
  - Added term_tracker_item pointing at issue #3522.

Signed-off-by: @dragon-ai-agent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Following @rays22's analysis, I corrected the term so that:

- `def`: "A foramen in the septum primum." (was "septum secundum")
- logical def: `part_of` [UBERON:0004154 atrial septum primum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004154) (was `UBERON:0004155 atrial septum secundum`)

The NCIT mapping (`NCIT:C34179 Foramen Secundum`) on the Uberon term is left in place; the issue with the reversed `Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of` axiom on the NCIT side will need to be raised separately with NCIT, but the Uberon class is now anatomically correct independent of that NCIT axiom.

Changes committed in PR #<NN>.

input.content:
## Summary

Fixes obophenotype/uberon#3522: corrects the reversed/incorrect `part_of` relationship and text definition on [`UBERON:0006678` foramen secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006678).

## Background

The issue reporter noted that Uberon and NCIT disagree on the direction of the `part_of` relationship between the foramen secundum and the septum secundum. A subsequent comment by @rays22 (Uberon MEMBER) identified that **both** assertions were wrong, and that the correct anatomy is:

> The foramen secundum is an opening in the **septum primum**, not the septum secundum.

This is supported by:

1. The cited Wikipedia article on [Foramen secundum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foramen_secundum): *"the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen in the septum primum."*
2. The own definition of [`UBERON:0004154` atrial septum primum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004154) (already in Uberon): *"...perforations appear in the superior part of the septum primum, forming the ostium secundum."*
3. The pre-existing `external_definition` on `UBERON:0006678` itself: *"Second of the two openings to perforate the septum primum between the two atria of the embryonic heart."* — i.e. the text definition was already inconsistent with the term's own annotations, and only the logical axiom and the `def:` line needed to be brought in line with the rest of the annotations.
4. The medical anatomy textbook (ISBN: 9632412311) cited by @rays22.

This means the change here brings the term into internal consistency rather than introducing a new anatomical claim.

## Changes to `UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum`

| Field | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `def` | "A foramen in the septum secundum." | "A foramen in the septum primum." |
| `intersection_of: part_of` | `UBERON:0004155 ! atrial septum secundum` | `UBERON:0004154 ! atrial septum primum` |
| `term_tracker_item` | (absent) | `https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522` |

The `xref: NCIT:C34179` (Foramen Secundum) is retained — it is a correct class-level mapping. The reversed NCIT `Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of` axiom called out in the issue is an upstream NCIT issue and out of scope for Uberon to fix directly.

## Checklist

- [x] Read the issue and the follow-up MEMBER comment in full.
- [x] Verified `UBERON:0004154` (atrial septum primum) exists and is the correct target.
- [x] Verified `UBERON:0004155` (atrial septum secundum) is a sibling under `UBERON:0002085 ! interatrial septum`, confirming both are valid `is_a interatrial septum` classes (so the old part_of axiom was a relationship error, not a class error).
- [x] Checked all other references to `UBERON:0006678` in `uberon-edit.obo`: only one other axiom (`UBERON:0004754 disjoint_from UBERON:0006678`) refers to it, and that axiom remains correct.

## Summary

Fixes obophenotype/uberon#3522: corrects the reversed/incorrect `part_of` relationship and text definition on [`UBERON:0006678` foramen secundum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006678).

## Background

The issue reporter noted that Uberon and NCIT disagree on the direction of the `part_of` relationship between the foramen secundum and the septum secundum. A subsequent comment by @rays22 (Uberon MEMBER) identified that **both** assertions were wrong, and that the correct anatomy is:

> The foramen secundum is an opening in the **septum primum**, not the septum secundum.

This is supported by:

1. The cited Wikipedia article on [Foramen secundum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foramen_secundum): *"the foramen secundum or ostium secundum is a foramen in the septum primum."*
2. The own definition of [`UBERON:0004154` atrial septum primum](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004154) (already in Uberon): *"...perforations appear in the superior part of the septum primum, forming the ostium secundum."*
3. The pre-existing `external_definition` on `UBERON:0006678` itself: *"Second of the two openings to perforate the septum primum between the two atria of the embryonic heart."* — i.e. the text definition was already inconsistent with the term's own annotations, and only the logical axiom and the `def:` line needed to be brought in line with the rest of the annotations.
4. The medical anatomy textbook (ISBN: 9632412311) cited by @rays22.

This means the change here brings the term into internal consistency rather than introducing a new anatomical claim.

## Changes to `UBERON:0006678 foramen secundum`

| Field | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `def` | "A foramen in the septum secundum." | "A foramen in the septum primum." |
| `intersection_of: part_of` | `UBERON:0004155 ! atrial septum secundum` | `UBERON:0004154 ! atrial septum primum` |
| `term_tracker_item` | (absent) | `https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3522` |

The `xref: NCIT:C34179` (Foramen Secundum) is retained — it is a correct class-level mapping. The reversed NCIT `Anatomic_Structure_Is_Physical_Part_Of` axiom called out in the issue is an upstream NCIT issue and out of scope for Uberon to fix directly.

- [x] Checked all other references to `UBERON:0006678` in `uberon-edit.obo`: only one other axiom (`UBERON:0004754 disjoint_from UBERON:0006678`) refers to it, and that axiom remains correct.
